learnings.title: prettier-then-eslint-fix sequence
learnings.description: Run prettier --write first, then eslint --fix. Prettier reformats; eslint --fix adds missing curly braces where prettier split single-line ifs onto multiple lines. Two-step sequence resolves all conflicts without manual edits.
learnings.apply_when: Any bulk formatting pass on the codebase
learnings.avoid_when: Never run eslint --fix before prettier — order matters

learnings.title: prettierignore-before-format
learnings.description: Always create .prettierignore before running prettier --write. Scope away .vibe/, .gsd/, .jules/, *.jsonl, and any generated/state directories. Keeps the diff reviewable and prevents state file churn.
learnings.apply_when: First-time prettier setup on any project

learnings.title: prettier-breaks-digest-index
learnings.description: prettier --write reformats skill index.js files, changing their content and invalidating SHA-256 digests in .well-known/agent-skills/index.json. This fails the index-json-integrity harness check. Always run writeIndex() after any bulk formatting pass.
learnings.avoid_when: After any prettier --write pass that touches skills/*/index.js

learnings.title: npm-files-field-for-publish-safety
learnings.description: The 'files' field in package.json explicitly controls what gets published to npm. Without it, .vibe/ state, .husky/ hooks, test fixtures, and dev configs leak into the published package. Always list: bin/, lib/, skills/, catalog/, references/, .well-known/, and key docs.
learnings.apply_when: Any project with a publishable npm package

learnings.title: prod-only-npm-audit
learnings.description: npm audit --omit=dev separates real prod vulnerabilities from dev tooling noise. Jest internals regularly have moderate CVEs that cannot be fixed without --force. Running prod-only audit in CI prevents alert fatigue and gives a clean signal: 0 real vulnerabilities.
learnings.apply_when: CI security audit step for any Node.js project

learnings.title: retro-notes-string-vs-array
learnings.description: runRetro() returns notes as a string. retro.js iterated it as an array, printing individual characters ('A', 'l', 'l'). Fix: always coerce: Array.isArray(notes) ? notes : [String(notes)]. Root cause: implicit interface contract between auto-maintain.js and retro.js was never documented or tested.
evidence: retro output showing 18 items / A / l / l
learnings.avoid_when: When a function returns a value that callers treat as a specific type

learnings.title: ci-node-matrix
learnings.description: Run CI against Node 20 and Node 22 in a matrix. Catches version-specific behavior before it reaches users. Use actions/setup-node with cache: npm for fast installs. Pin .nvmrc to the LTS version (20) to align local dev with the lower CI bound.

learnings.title: pre-commit-index-integrity-gate
learnings.description: Adding checkIndexIntegrity() as Gate 3 in .husky/pre-commit catches digest drift before it hits the harness. Skill file edits that change content without rebuilding the index now fail at commit time with a clear fix command, not at CI time 10 minutes later.
learnings.apply_when: Any commit touching files under skills/*/index.js

learnings.title: tdd-before-optimization
learnings.description: Write tests for the function's observable behavior BEFORE optimizing its internals. Bolt iterated 3x on bin/vibe.js (commits 153e3a1, 3726dca, c21356e) because no tests guarded the behavior. With TTLCache and allowBacktrack tests in place first, future optimizations have a safety net.
learnings.apply_when: Before any performance optimization or internal refactor

learnings.title: backlog-review-by-date
learnings.description: Rules with 0 fires should not be deleted immediately — add review_by dates (30 days from creation). At review time: if still 0 fires, retire. If fires > 0, mark quality_score and keep. Prevents premature deletion of rules that haven't had a chance to fire.
learnings.apply_when: When adding new rules that describe future-state behavior

learnings.title: skill-key-namespace-mismatch
learnings.description: SKILL_TO_TEMPLATE was built with module-level names (tdd-vibe, security-audit, one-click-vercel) but SkillRouter.route() returns vibe-* CLI names (vibe-tdd, vibe-security, vibe-deploy). The entire adaptive template selection feature was a silent no-op — _selectTemplate() always returned null, no test failed, no warning was emitted. Root cause: the map was written by inspecting skills/ directory names rather than SkillRouter's ROUTING_TABLE output.
learnings.avoid_when: When building any map keyed on runtime output values — always trace the actual value from the source (SkillRouter, event system, API) rather than inferring from adjacent artifacts
learnings.fix: Replace keys with the actual vibe-* names; add a test asserting all keys start with vibe- to catch future drift

learnings.title: object-as-span-attribute
learnings.description: span.setAttribute('template', enriched.selectedTemplate) passed a raw {category, id, skill} object. The child enricher span formatted it as a string 'testing/full'. This creates an inconsistent OTel span schema — same attribute name, different types — breaking any consumer that queries spans.jsonl. OTel span attributes must be primitives (string, number, boolean).
learnings.avoid_when: When calling setAttribute() with a value derived from an object — always coerce: `${obj.field}` or JSON.stringify(obj) or a dedicated primitive field

learnings.title: duplicated-cooldown-constant
learnings.description: bin/vibe.js and .vibe/lifecycle/auto-maintain.js both hardcoded a cooldown value but used different numbers (60s vs 30s). A direct invocation of auto-maintain.js could fire within the 60s window that bin/vibe.js set as the guard. Root cause: no shared constant, no cross-file test. Fix: align to 60s in both files. Long-term: extract to lifecycle.json triggers config.
learnings.avoid_when: When the same business rule (cooldown, threshold, timeout) is enforced in multiple files — define it once and import, or read from shared config

learnings.title: vibe-review-catches-silent-bugs
learnings.description: A quick 3-persona vibe-review (CEO+CTO+Security) on a 408-line diff caught 1 blocker (object span attribute corrupting spans.jsonl) and the SKILL_TO_TEMPLATE namespace mismatch that made template selection permanently invisible. Both bugs had passing tests and passing harness. Review personas surface cross-file semantic consistency that unit tests cannot.
learnings.apply_when: Before every commit touching orchestration or telemetry code — these have implicit cross-file contracts that tests don't cover

learnings.title: incomplete-unicode-sanitization
learnings.description: _sanitize() filtered ASCII control chars (charCode < 32) but allowed all Unicode, including U+202E RIGHT-TO-LEFT OVERRIDE, U+200B ZERO WIDTH SPACE, and U+200F RIGHT-TO-LEFT MARK. These chars are ≥ 32 so passed the existing filter. They enable visual deception — injected text appears blank or reversed in logs but is injected into LLM context. Fix: explicitly reject U+200B–U+200F, U+202A–U+202E, U+FEFF.
learnings.avoid_when: Any text sanitizer for LLM injection prevention — the charCode >= 32 heuristic is insufficient; Unicode BiDi and zero-width chars are vectors
learnings.fix: Filter: if (cp >= 0x200B && cp <= 0x200F) return false; if (cp >= 0x202A && cp <= 0x202E) return false; if (cp === 0xFEFF) return false;

learnings.title: test-keys-against-runtime-source
learnings.description: When testing a lookup map, the test must use the same keys the runtime will use — not keys inferred from adjacent artifacts. A test checking SKILL_TO_TEMPLATE['tdd-vibe'] === 'testing' passes even when SkillRouter never produces 'tdd-vibe'. Add a structural test: assert all keys match the format of the actual source (e.g., all keys start with 'vibe-'). This would have caught the namespace mismatch at test-write time.
learnings.apply_when: Any time you write tests for a map/enum keyed on external identifiers
